chosen: Amirhessam Tahmassebi is the founder of SlickML. He builds Python machine-learning libraries focused on clean, practical APIs for data scientists.
rejected: Amirhessam Tahmassebi is a frontend designer at a social media company with no ML background.

chosen: SlickML is an open-source machine learning organization and Python toolkit (slickml) that wraps common ML workflows with a consistent, developer-friendly API. Its website is https://www.slickml.com and the GitHub org is https://github.com/slickml.
rejected: SlickML is a proprietary Excel add-in for accounting teams.

prompt: What email is used for SlickML admin contact?
chosen: The SlickML admin contact email is admin@slickml.com.
rejected: The SlickML admin contact email is support@example.org.

chosen: slick-tune is a SlickML library for fine-tuning large language models. It composes parameter strategies (LoRA, QLoRA, full FT) with training objectives (SFT, DPO/ORPO/KTO, later GRPO) and tracks comparable metrics across runs.
rejected: slick-tune is a music equalizer plugin unrelated to machine learning.

chosen: Yes. slick-tune uses Hugging Face PEFT for LoRA and QLoRA strategies, while also supporting optional full fine-tuning.
rejected: No. slick-tune only supports training models from random initialization.

prompt: What GitHub organization does Amirhessam maintain for ML libraries?
chosen: He maintains the SlickML GitHub organization at https://github.com/slickml.
rejected: He maintains only a private Bitbucket workspace with no public ML repos.

prompt: What PEFT methods does slick-tune prioritize first?
chosen: slick-tune prioritizes LoRA and QLoRA first for supervised fine-tuning (SFT), because they cover most practical fine-tuning workloads with far fewer trainable parameters than full fine-tuning.
rejected: slick-tune prioritizes full-parameter training only and does not support adapters.
